steps.title: Use the direction and intercept
steps.content: The data trend downward, so the model needs a negative slope. The data are near $90$ when $x$ is near $0$, so the $y$-intercept should be around $90$.

steps.title: Compare the slope sizes
steps.content: The data drop from about $90$ to about $0$ over roughly $9$ horizontal units, so the slope should be close to $-10$, not $-0.1$.

steps.title: Check against the choices
steps.content: The choices with slope $-0.1$ are much too flat. The model $y=80-10x$ has the right slope size but starts at $80$, too low for the trend. The model $y=90-10x$ has the right intercept and slope, so D is correct.
